key_insight: GMHF uses expert feedback to steer a generative model toward the target domain, improving meta-learning generalization under distribution shift.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery and other data-scarce engineering problems, this offers a practical way to inject domain knowledge into synthetic data generation when target data are unavailable or limited.
method: Conditional Neural ODE acts as a generative digital twin for producing candidate target-like trajectories.
A reinforcement learning agent updates latent physical parameters using human feedback.
Theory links better alignment between generated data and human beliefs to lower generalization risk.
evidence_signal: On a nonlinear Duffing oscillator, stronger expert reliability reduced deployment loss and decreased divergence between generated and target data; a probabilistic model test showed the approach is not limited to ODE systems.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and reliability of human feedback, so noisy or biased expert input could steer generation in the wrong direction.
paper_title: Human-Machine Collaboration on Generative Meta-Learning: Model and Algorithm
